title: Deliciously Steamed Greek Bok Choi Loukoumades
description: Bitter, snappy, comforting Greek dish featuring bok choi, simmered in traditional Greek flavors without added sugars. Serve in scallop shells for an elegant presentation.
instructions: Rinse the bok choi under cold running water, and trim off the ends.
Cut the bok choi into wedges.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and saut1 until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the bok choi and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and slightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the bok choi to a large bowl and stir in the lemon juice, vinegar, honey, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Place a small amount of the bok choi mixture into each scallop shell.
Create the loukoumades by piping a ring of dough around the bok choi, making sure not to seal the center.
Fry the loukoumades in hot oil until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Drain the loukoumades on paper towels.
Serve the loukoumades in the scallop shells, topped with a dollop of Greek yogurt if desired.
Garnish with lemon wedges and a sprinkle of paprika, if desired.
